query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity:

**Latest weekly/recent trend (June 2026):**
- **Final week of June was extreme volatility** — the week of June 22–26 saw the KOSPI fall ~10% with two circuit breakers, driven by heavy foreign selling: ~5.80 trillion won net sell (June 23) and ~4.62 trillion won (June 26).
- **June 29 set an all-time single-day record**: foreign net sell of **7.7332 trillion won**.
- Selling ran for 8 consecutive trading days (June 19–30).

**Monthly & half-year context:**
- **June 2026**: −48.62 trillion won on KOSPI — largest monthly net sell ever.
- **May 2026**: ~−47 trillion won (all listed) / −49.41 trillion won KOSPI-only.
- **H1 2026 total**: ~**148–149 trillion won net sell** — the largest "Sell Korea" on record, over 10x the full-year 2025 figure of ~11 trillion won.

**Key nuance:** This selling occurred *despite* the KOSPI rallying ~100%+ YTD (AI/semiconductor boom in Samsung & SK Hynix). Both KRX and analysts frame it as **mechanical portfolio rebalancing/profit-taking**, not lost confidence — with foreign ownership share still hitting record highs (~40.7%) because valuation gains outpaced selling. Domestic retail investors absorbed the outflows (net +99 trillion won H1). Analysts quoted expect selling pressure to persist into H2 2026.

There was one brief interruption: June 12 saw foreign net buying of ~1.7 trillion won (first in ~25 sessions) during a rebound, before selling resumed.

All data is sourced to KRX, FSS, Yonhap, Korea Herald, and other outlets, with the most recent hard data through June 30, 2026.
